title: The Reach of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard
source: The Guardian - The Week USA – April 10, 2026
content:
Officially, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) is the military force sworn to protect Iran’s ruling clerics. However, its enormous reach into all aspects of political and economic life effectively makes it a powerful state within a state. Far better resourced than Iran’s regular armed forces, the IRGC controls roughly half of the country’s $376 billion economy and directs the nation's nuclear program. In response to crippling international sanctions, the IRGC has developed a widespread "resistance economy" characterized by intricate illicit trade networks. This economic dominance ensures the organization remains fiercely loyal to the regime while continuously expanding its own power base.

Beyond its domestic economic and military influence, the IRGC serves as the primary engine for exporting Iran's ideology and geopolitical influence. The organization maintains an extensive network of proxy forces that frequently target both regional adversaries and international targets. For instance, in 1994, a devastating bombing killed 85 people at a Jewish community center in Buenos Aires, an attack reportedly planned by current IRGC commander General Ahmad Vahidi. More recently, the force systematically trained and equipped Hamas militants in Gaza ahead of the October 7, 2023, attacks on Israel. Furthermore, authorities linked IRGC-plotted arson attacks to incidents at Jewish institutions in Australia during 2024.

The organization's structure includes several distinct wings, each designed to manage specific domestic and international responsibilities. The notorious Quds Force operates primarily abroad, cultivating relationships with allied militias and conducting clandestine intelligence operations. Domestically, the Basij paramilitary force is mobilized to violently suppress dissent, enforce strict social codes, and break up anti-government protests. Through these varied components, the IRGC maintains an iron grip on Iranian society while simultaneously projecting immense power across the broader Middle East. Its dual role as both an economic powerhouse and a military enforcer makes the IRGC the central pillar of the current Iranian regime.
